scaffold.description: Create a new opencode skill. Prompts for name and description, then writes SKILL.md.
scaffold.template: Walk the user through creating a new skill. Ask for the skill name (lowercase, hyphen-separated, up to 64 chars) and a one-sentence description that covers what the skill does AND when to trigger it. Create the directory .opencode/skills/<name>/ and write a SKILL.md file with frontmatter (name, description) and a helpful body with instructions, examples, and references as appropriate. Then remind the user to restart opencode for the new skill to be loaded.

council.description: Ask the LLM Council for a multi-perspective answer. Use when user asks about philosophy, advice, tradeoffs, vision, or wants a balanced deliberation. Trigger: /council <question>
council.template: The user has triggered /council. Strip the /council prefix from the message to extract the question. Call the council API with `curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/council -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"message": "<question>"}' --max-time 120`. Parse the JSON response. Present the Chairman's synthesis (stage3) as the primary answer, formatted as a well-structured response. Optionally mention which agents contributed if the synthesis doesn't cover it. Do NOT show raw agent responses — only the synthesized answer. If the council is unavailable or the request fails, respond that the council is not available.
